back_translation: Moses assembled the whole congregation of the sons of Israel and said to them, 'These are the words that the LORD has commanded to do.'
'Six days work shall be done, but on the seventh day there shall be a holy day for you — a Sabbath of complete rest to the LORD. Whoever does any work on it shall be put to death.'
'You shall not kindle a fire in any of your dwellings on the Sabbath day.'
Moses spoke to the whole congregation of the sons of Israel, saying, 'This is the word that the LORD has commanded, saying:'
'Take from among you an offering to the LORD. Everyone whose heart is willing — let him bring it as the LORD's offering: gold, silver, and bronze,'
'blue, purple, and scarlet yarn, fine linen, and goat hair,'
'ram skins dyed red, fine leather, and acacia wood,'
'oil for the light, spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense,'
'and onyx stones and stones for setting on the ephod and the breastpiece.'
'Every skilled of heart among you, let them come and make all that the LORD has commanded:'
'the tabernacle, its tent and its covering, its clasps and its frames, its crossbars, its pillars, and its bases,'
'the ark and its poles, the mercy seat, and the veil of the screen,'
'the table and its poles and all its vessels and the Bread of the Presence,'
'the lampstand for light and its vessels and its lamps and the oil for the light,'
'the altar of incense and its poles and the anointing oil and the fragrant incense and the screen for the entrance, for the entrance of the tabernacle,'
'the altar of burnt offering and its bronze grate and its poles and all its vessels, the basin and its stand,'
'the hangings of the courtyard, its pillars and its bases, and the screen of the gate of the courtyard,'
'the pegs of the tabernacle and the pegs of the courtyard and their cords,'
'the woven garments to minister in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons to serve as priests.'
The whole congregation of the sons of Israel went out from before Moses.

Everyone whose heart stirred him and everyone whose spirit prompted him came and brought the offering of the LORD for the work of the Tent of Meeting and for all its service and for the holy garments.
The men came with the women — all the willing of heart — they brought brooches, earrings, rings, and necklaces, all articles of gold; everyone who waved a wave offering of gold to the LORD.
Everyone with whom was found blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goat hair, ram skins dyed red, or fine leather, brought them.
Everyone offering a contribution of silver and bronze brought it as the LORD's offering. And everyone with whom was found acacia wood for any work of the service brought it.
Every skilled woman spun with her hands and brought what she had spun — blue, purple, scarlet, and fine linen.
And all the women whose hearts stirred them with skill spun the goat hair.
The leaders brought onyx stones and stones for setting, for the ephod and for the breastpiece,'
'and the spice and the oil — for the light, for the anointing oil, and for the fragrant incense.'
Every man and woman whose heart prompted them brought to bring for all the work that the LORD had commanded by the hand of Moses to do; the sons of Israel brought a freewill offering to the LORD.
Moses said to the sons of Israel, 'See, the LORD has called by name Bezalel son of Uri son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah.'
'He has filled him with the Spirit of God, with wisdom, with understanding, with knowledge, and with all craftsmanship,'
'and to devise designs, to work in gold and in silver and in bronze,'
'and in cutting stones for setting, and in carving wood, to do every artistic work.'
'And to teach he has given in his heart — him and Oholiab son of Ahisamach of the tribe of Dan.'

'He has filled them with skill of heart to do every work of an engraver and a designer and an embroiderer in blue and in purple, in scarlet and in fine linen, and a weaver — workers of every work and designers of designs.'
